Prior ProvisionsA prior section 3782, , ; , , , provided for boards of inquiry, composed of three or more officers, to be convened at such places as Secretary of the Army prescribes, to receive evidence and make findings and recommendations whether an officer, required to show cause under , should be retained on active list of Regular Army, prior to repeal by , title VII, § 701, , , 2955, effective . children.text: reasonable grounds exist to question the current validity of the asserted restriction; and
the continued adherence by the United States to the asserted restriction would make it impracticable to procure the item to which the technical data pertain competitively at a later time.

children.heading: Time Limit for Challenges; Exceptions.—
children.label: b Time Limit for Challenges; Exceptions.—
B
children.text: have been furnished to the United States without restriction;

children.label: C
children.text: have been otherwise made available without restriction; or

children.label: D
children.text: are the subject of a fraudulently asserted use or release restriction.

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which final payment is made on the contract under which the technical data are required to be delivered; or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the technical data are delivered under the contract.

children.heading: .—Written Notice to Contractor or Subcontractor
children.label: c .—Written Notice to Contractor or Subcontractor

children.text: state the specific grounds for challenging the asserted restriction;
require a response within 60 days justifying the current validity of the asserted restriction; and
state that evidence of a justification described in subsection (d) may be submitted.
such validation occurred after a challenge to the validated restriction under this section; and
the validated restriction was asserted by the same contractor or subcontractor (or a licensee of such contractor or subcontractor).
